title: Typed Bucket Endpoint and S3 Express Compatibility · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs typed bucket endpoint and s3 express compatibility from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript General Directory Bucket · changed case · Review General Directory B…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8564 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow bucket type before validating directory-bucket endpoint and session state; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

lines.label: assumption

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Bucket Naming Creation Gate, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Bucket Naming Creation Gate record says bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Bucket Naming Creation Gate.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Bucket Naming Creation Gate without its stated preconditions, omit the bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Bucket Naming Creation Gate for bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.
